columns.extraction_plan: The abstract on page 1 states: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." The "INTRODUCTION" section on page 1 refers to this as "early treatment intensification with a combination of androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus either docetaxel or an androgen receptor pathway inhibitor". The "Relevance" section on page 2 explicitly calls it "Triplet therapy". Therefore, the treatment class can be categorized as **Triplet therapy (Androgen receptor pathway inhibitor + ADT + Docetaxel)**.
